messages.text: We tried changing the recipe and HMI froze up. Maintenance is going to do a hard reset.
Line remains down. Maintenance continues to troubleshoot.
We changed the recipe and we are still seeing the same issue.

Preston Elmer 
Have you reached out to TG for a call in the morning?
 
 
Itzel Chavez 
Friday night issues with TG 4& 5
I am not sure how many molds it takes to fill the gondola, only run enough bars to fill the gondola + 50 more molds.
 
Send 1/2 to TG 4 then 1/2 to TG 5.
 
Empty the WDS. 
 
I reached out to Matt asking if he could speak to a TG tech so a tech can call Preston in the morning. 
 
 
If you could reach out to Felix or any other tech you have a number for that would be great.
 
 
If a TG tech responds post here, so we know what time someone can help us.
 
I will be in early so we can go over the plan, or if you have a different idea please let me know.
 
 
 
I hope you had a wonderful Mother's Day ☺️

Thank you for sending Rose Smuin. 

Added you to the TG Technical Support so you can see the back and forth.

Good Morning,
We are tempering on both units, but when recirculating molds we have had 4 mold jams as of now on different spots.
-Before cold stamp 
-Conveyors after drum inserter
-After cooling cabinet 2
-Preheating Cabinet. 

Also, south swecos were installed upside down again.
Itzel Chavez were you able to get support from Theegarten? I had the tech looking to support this morning.
No yet, is he doing a teams call or something, or it’s via email?
Depositor 1 Centerlines checklist and shadow board
Depositor 2 Centerlines and checklist
Depositor 3 centerlines and checklist
Depositor 4 checklist
Ptls checklist and shadow board

Did somebody on your team reach out for request? I wasn't included on anything so I just asked the guy on site at the show to reach out to their service and let them know to be responsive when the request come through.
I believe Preston reached out.
I have reached out to Konstantin, haven’t received an answer yet.
We are depositing.
Preston did reach out at 11:34pm yesterday to make the request on the TG Technical chat. 👍
Depósitor 3 in the range

We are depositing 400 molds only,
Gondola will get full with around 300 molds, and will do 100 more to leave them in cooling cabinet 4 for testing.
Depositor 3 in the range

Itzel Chavez Chad Stone Rose Smuin Matt Hatch There is a teams call at 7am with TG. I'll have a headset out on the machines not in a conference room. Justin Halladay might be able to come over but he's dealing with a bad jam over there in L2. I'm currently going over a couple documents that Julia from TG sent me for the issues.
Lot end date is correct
Tempering chart check update
Are we working with the operator to bring that down?
Organoleptic check update

Thank you Itzel Chavez

Let’s also ensure we start up TG3 to know there are not any issues. 

The goal would be to 

- Have TG3 run 200-300 bars to know that it works

- Have the Gondola full so we can troubleshoot TG4 & TG5

- Have the WDS empty

This way as soon as Preston gets TG 4 or TG5 back to standard we start ML4 back up at full speed (it is empty so you don’t have to pause for anything)
weight check update
Yes
He has already made adjustments to the temperature.
Those look like they bloomed from that early temper spike. Do we have ee's watching and pulling them off until it goes away?

This adjustment sensor cable was broken off at the plug. Luckily the damage is minimal but this is something that will keep a flow wrapper down for hours if it was worse. Please have your teams be careful. Itzel Chavez Tyson Fausett Kimberly Morales Nathan Gevenois Rich Brierley Marcos Montano Alan Greer Rose Smuin
Dimensions check update
Yip: We already have a coworker doing that procedure with the bad chocolate.
On TG4, the button—which indicates where to make the cut—was switched off. The air was positioned too far back; the operator returned it to its proper place.

These changes had been made on Tg5; when the chocolate arrived, it began jamming before entering the TG. The operator switched the settings back to how we had left them on Friday, and it started packaging without any issues.
Thank you for the updates, Alba!☺️
TG4 working without any problems.
TG5 working without any problems.

Itzel Chavez Tyson Fausett  Theegarten 4 and 5 started up this morning with very little issues. I had their team on a conference call and they were ready to assist but none of the problems from Friday night were present.

Thank you for communicating Preston Elmer

Did the TG4 motor cause any more issues?

Itzel Chavez,

Have we started depositing at 25 MPM?
Yes we started at 7:30am depositing full speed 25mpm.

We are down due to the Gondola showing this alarm 

Preston Elmer is here
Packaging display box check update

Motor# S4M42
Had a loose roller. Preston is adjusting it.
This alarm it’s something with the pushers for TG #4, Preston is still troubleshooting.
1 of the gondola carriages is bent.
Preston said when it comes back around we can swap it for a good one.
Organoleptic check update The product's appearance looks much better.

Tempering chart check update 


The temperature of the enrober was regulated and falls within the parameters required by the centerline.
Which machine was this?
TG4
We are still down due to the Gondola, Preston is getting in contact with TG right now to troubleshoot.
That’s good to hear. The only thing that happened to TG4 on Friday night was the splitter over load at the end of the shift
TG#3 belt was not properly assembled

Still down,
TG is telling Preston to do a reference run on the Gondola, he’s waiting on instructions on how to do it.
Doing reference run now.
Back up and running at 10:20am.

Good morning, the hopper heat was off and the chocolate in the hopper became hard/cold. I checked the shutdown checklist to verify if it had been completed, and it was signed off. Step 4 states to leave the hopper heat ON and set it to 120°F. It is important to carefully verify each shutdown step before signing off the checklist.
An adjustment was made to the mixing water, but the water jacket is not remaining stable; Maintenance has been called to inspect it.
We had one good Temper at 0.7. Took another temper and result was 1.1
The metal detector belt moved
Maintenance is working on it
Lot and date is correct
We are currently still waiting on temper and making adjustments as needed
Screen free of damage and clean
Robert Villarreal
On Chris's recommendation, the machine is set to Reheat at 7:30.
That's not reheat. That's night mode.
Starting reheat now.
metal detector belt is moved again
Maintenance fixed that
The recipe has started.
Update on action taken for temper since 6:02 am
Two good tempers have come out; we are waiting for a third temper to get started.
The metal detector is constantly activating and opening, the conveyor belt is moving. Maintenance has been checking it.
9:15 we started depositing
Maintenance is currently working on the metal detector
Maintenance continues working on the detector belt; if it does not function correctly, the line is stopped.
The metal detector is opening constantly, and maintenance is working on it.
We stopped the line because the belt is still moving.
Metal detector belt has shifted maintenance is making adjustments
We lowered the mixing water to 83.2
Let's empty the hopper
Who’s responsible for the contractors working today?  Matt Hatch Jorge Carrillo Marco Rodriguez
Marco Rodriguez  for anything happening on Line 1.
Is there something we need
I spoke with Nick on Friday about it, he was having some trouble getting it from the supplier but is hopeful it will be here sometime this week. I will follow up with him again today. Thanks.
The secondary door must always remain closed please!

Good morning, team. We encountered a damaged chocolate flow valve—the one running from the buffer to the enrobers. We made the necessary adjustments, but it is still not functioning. We require assistance from the mechanics.
We have also secured the separator table, accompanied by a constant noise.
The guillotine isn't working.
We are waiting for maintenance to arrive.
The buffer was found full and overflowing.
Preston Elmer, Nathan Ruiz Nelson Cook
Clean buffer area
Right now, the mechanic is working on the valve.
The bottomer isn't working; the main scraper is likely down.
We need the Bottomer lever of the enrober to be completely closed because when it is opened, the main scraper falls immediately; for that reason, it is noted in the centerline
Lot and date is correct
Return line valve is now operational the buffer tank was at 107% and is currently being drained into the set points so the valve will work in auto mode.
Guillotine is now operational the servo drive was faulted out
Thank you Nathan Ruiz
The line started at 9:14am
What was the issue with the valve?

Matt Hatch
the buffer tank was at 107% it would not let it turn on.
But do we know why it went to 107%?
Pre  piece in the range
The temper reached 1.5, so we stopped the line.
Temper is currently at 1.9
Good first temper
Rose Smuin Preston Elmer Rich Brierley During removal of chocolate from the line a broken cover conveyor cover was found
Happy Monday 😊

These covers were a custom request to replace the big bulky fiberglass ones that keep breaking because they are so big and heavy. There is no air cooling on that tunnel, so it was really just to cover the product. But if QA would rather get rid of items that will break, we can just leave the conveyor uncovered. It shouldn't affect the product or functionality of the cooling belt.

▓ 
A Clean and Rezero operation was being performed on Matrix 3 when red brush bristles fell in. The belts are now being cleaned in preparation for the ATP procedure. Katya Farnsworth
We do not have a tempering sheet for the 10226 for the decorator. Can we please make one? Robert Villarreal Erandy Garcilazo Ceja
If these brushes are coming apart, thrown them away and we’ll get new ones. Katya Farnsworth

The weights board has been created.


There is no spec of what the pre piece weight needs to be. Without the pre piece weight range, we are unable to define the chocolate usage range or target and the spec does not show it. However, I should be able to document the chocolate usage once we know the pre piece and final piece weights. There just won't be a spec of what the minimun/ maximum choc usage should be. Justin Katya Charity
Good catch S3 Enrober (Jesus)
Thank you for posting this Jesus!  Miriah Manzanares,Candon Watt,Rafael Baretta can we get this information added to the spec please.
Justin Garner can you share the item number for this FG
Matrix West Level 2 Cleaning This ready

Justin Garner there is only 1  pretzel weight historical in safety chain- from June 2025. It looks like that production run has 2 piece weight checks, and both pretzel weight ranges are 1.7-1.8, with final weight 5.6. 
When Candon and Rafael arrive, I will F2F with them on how much more 'trend' data is needed to assign a pretzel weight spec. Mary Montilla continue to gather pre-piece weights by taking a weight for every lot used, then immediately giving that weight information to production to adjust their chocolate usage to achieve the target 5.73g
Line is running Justin Garner
Level 2 cleaning and visual inspection are performed in Matrix and Low Mars bands of the Matrix, ruling out any type of contamination.
Enrober and decorator tempering chart and Enrober CL.
Centerline and Yamato MM3 MM4 MM5

We paused the line because we can not hit the piece count in pouches. We were getting only 76-80 pieces.
QA is doing individual piece weights and we have increased dark chocolate (10227) temper to 0.7 to see if that helps. After temper reaches desired temp, we will run a section to see if that helps Justin Garner
Shaker 90% and the blower at 98%
CW MM3 MM4 MM5
The last time we ran this product we were approved to raise CW weight to allow for more product. Miriah Manzanares Charity Young Justin Garner

Katya Farnsworth can you screenshot the run rules deviation from March, here? 
There is some confusion on the total piece count approved
I can not find the message that says it was approved to have 85pcs, this is all I can find on weight increase on Checkweigher though

Thank you. 
We have approval from Kathleen Gilbert that 85 is acceptable. 
But weMUST ensure we are not UNDERWEIGHT on any. 
Curt Willis
Justin Garner
Katya Farnsworth
Mary Montilla
Charity Young
Maintenance just inspected the Enrober's shaker belt to verify it was not loose. They said it looked good, so no adjustments were made. Justin, Katya
Can we match the checkweigher settings from the last run then? Miriah Manzanares Charity Young
Enrober temper is now at 0.7

That decision will be championed by production, approved by quality .
I understand there’s some levers to pull that production has, Mary Montilla will need to know what the piece count and weights are and approve from there. (Zero underweights)
I believe though, Curt Willis has the intent to  increase CW to ensure 85pieces and package weight is achieved.
I will communicate to QA.
Line is back up and running.
After the adjustments we have 94 pieces total. 3 doubles and 6 broken
Justin Garner
What is the target for the chocolate tempering?
94 pieces per pouch
Enrober is currently at 0.7, and the decorator is at 0.8.
We do not have a tempering chart for this product with this chocolate. We are using 10227 dark with the target of 0.5, but we increased it to 0.7 to help with chocolate Curt Willis
Change  the weight

Thanks Katya Farnsworth.
On your next check can you take a picture of the bottoms. Just curious how they look.
The authorized weight was changed to Q8.
I will need to wait for a few minutes for the change to come out.
The operator has just been informed to make adjustments, as the center pieces are arriving open at the bottom.
Thank you very much—the bottom part of the final piece has improved.
Justin Garner
The line started running at a speed of 65; right now, it has increased to 67.

Alex Mendoza,Jeffrey Barney

Please make sure this area is cleaned up of the rework before starting production. 

We can’t afford to get this mixed in when we startup.
The box printer is not printing the lot, maintenance is working on it
Line is running
The pouches looks good 👍🏻
18 pieces per pouch: on target 🎯
Lot verified
Check weighter is blowing the pouches from the line
Line is back up
Line is down due to the check weigher blowing everything off again.

Check weigher was reset and working.

QA is running the pouches that were blown off back through
Line is down we're having the same problem with the box printer

Pouch scrap report.

Alan ToddJeffrey BarneyAlex MendozaSofia CarcamoNidia Lopez Corea
Water test results: pass ✔️
Is this on ML2 or ML3 Alex Mendoza?
A total of 749 pieces were counted. No empty wraps, doubles, or open wraps were found.
Sorry ML2
Good morning! I’m seeing 102,000 pieces of CNC Item 21136 on hold. Will these be released? I’d like to know so I can keep my numbers accurate. Hervin Herrera, Alan Todd
Are these from the first day’s run Hervin Herrera?
Yes those are the pallets on hold that were put on hold the first day
Those pallets will stay on Hold Maria Moron Quintero

Alex Mendoza & Brandon Lance, please keep in mind that produced pieces placed on hold are not counted in the countdown. On Molding Line 2, there are still 2,000,000 pieces left to produce, not including anything produced today. This is only for WIP. Once the WIP is completed, we will start with the Box 15lbs (FG). Thank you
the line started at 6:30

We are having problems with the combination of the matrix and the vibration of the scales, it is making many pauses and we have approximately 38 PPM but the machine speed is 43 PPM, maintenance is on the line
We received the C1 adjustment at 4.5, which caused the bag to fold when reaching the suction pickups. We set it to CL at 13.5 and it is working correctly.
Date and Lot is correct

We have been having problems with the buckets, the screen shows many errors, we have had several buckets open, the operator removes them and places them in the correct position but after a few minutes we have the problem again, the screen also shows errors on certain buckets but when checking them they are working correctly, this affects the amount being poured into the bag which sometimes results in several bags being re-injected
